meaning: adj.不谦虚的,不庄重的,下流的
examples: At the risk of being immodest, I was a great salesman.
Students should avoid any immodest behaviour, on or off campus.
Tell me, child. Was this touch immodest?
Estimates of Ireland's embrace of tweeting vary from the robust to the immodest.
And on his modest pay he must pay immodest taxes (see story, page 62).
